steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for a choice that recounts the sequence of events the researchers proposed. That means the answer must include all three stages — in order: Chrysalis approached Saturn roughly 160 million years ago, tidal forces broke it apart, and the debris became Saturn’s rings.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘debris from Saturn’s rings would likely have caused Chrysalis to break apart’: reverses cause and effect — the debris formed the rings; the rings did not cause the breakup. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Saturn was once orbited by a large moon that broke apart’: omits the 160-million-year timing and the formation of the rings. Not a full sequence. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Around 160 million years ago, a large moon (Chrysalis) may have come close enough to Saturn that tidal forces broke the moon apart; its debris then formed the planet’s rings’: covers all three steps in the correct order — timing, breakup by tidal forces, rings formed from debris. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘If a moon orbiting a planet comes close enough, tidal forces can cause the moon to break apart’: states only the general principle from note 1; never mentions Chrysalis, the timing, or the rings. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
